As you hung up the phone, you felt a slight tingle run down your spine. You shivered and shook your shoulders, and then felt an odd feeling of waking up, which was strange as you'd been awake for hours. You'd gone to Tim's house, and then the hairdressers, and....oh god. The memories of what you'd done poured to the forefront of your mind. You ran to the mirror and your eyes widened:

         "Oh my God, what have I done to myself?" You said, touching the short blond hair which had replaced your long, black locks.

         "I look sexy," you frowned as the words left your lips.

         "That's not what I meant to say, I meant to say that I look really sexy" You clap your hands over your mouth. What was going on...

         "I. Look. Ssss. I. Look. Like. Tim's mother.the bombshell" you struggled to say the first words but the last came blurting out, unbidden.

         "I really need to get a tattoo," the thought entered your head and as it did, you felt a strong desire you'd never felt before.

         You hated tattoos, thought they looked crude and ruined people's skin. But now, deep inside your core, you heard a voice yelling "get one, get one!" The impulse was so powerful, you had to grasp a hold of the mirror's edge to stop yourself from falling over. The new desire pushed away all your worry over what had happened to your hair, and you rocked back and forth slightly as years of treating your body as a pristine temple came crashing against these new alien thoughts of reveling in the corruption of said temple.

Let's go!"

         Then an odd thing happened. It was as if the world became slightly muted. Your vision became slightly blurry, fuzzy and unfocused. You felt kind of tired. You tried to rub your eyes, but your arms responded slowly.

         "I wonder what kind of tattoo I should get today?" You said, though you thought the words didn't seem quite right, You shook your head and laughed and then walked into the kitchen. On the kitchen table, you saw your mother's bag, and you grinned.

         "Well, no point using *my* money to fund this little spree," you smirked as you checked the coast was clear, then reached into your mother's bag and pulled out her purse. Slowly, I felt something again.

"… " I heard something buzzing.

That buzzing became a ringing. That ringing became a siren. And finally, that siren became a scream.

"Tracy!"

I heard it clearly this time. 'Tracy', why did that voice call me that?

As slow and calm as that initial wave was, it suddenly turned into a raging beast. My senses crashed into me and threatened to overwhelm me as everything came at once, forcefully.

I suddenly found myself breathing in deeply, grasping for all the air I could get. My eyes trailed around me as if I was looking for an answer to a mystery I set up myself. Everything felt strange and foreign, and yet it felt so familiar all the same. My search came to an abrupt end when I met my reflection in the dark glass of the TV.

Even if it was only a brief encounter, it still managed to calm me down enough. Before my brain could fully realize what I saw, I felt a hand touching my exposed my shoulder.

"Tracy, hey, are you alright?"

I quickly turned around and came face to face with the guy I recognized immediately. Mixed emotions welled inside of me. He tried to put his hand on my forehead, caringly. For some odd reason, I didn't stop him from touching me. Something was wrong, and I needed to escape to recollect myself.

I told him...

What’s going on, let me go!” was just some of the minority of more formal protest language you yelled as you began being escorted out of the room and down the stairs to Tracy’s house. “Ms. Mason please calm down were here to help you, everything is going to be okay.” was the response from one of the men goose stepping you down the stairs as your arms hurt from the strain. Slamming through the door to the outside you are stunned to see a van for the local psychiatric institute parked in front of the house with three additional white shirts staring at you confrontational-like waiting for battle. With a stringent fear now enveloping your body and sprouting on the emotions etched in your face you look around in panic and see to the side the nerd you thought you could trust looking forlorn and dejected in deep emotional turmoil at you along with a crying older woman and depressed looking man beside her both looking a lot like Tracy. “Little shit I thought you were going to help me!” you scream at your punching bag as his eyes drop to the ground. You are grabbed and bundled from each side as the three men from the outside as well as the two who emerged from the house tackle you and hold you down facing up on the ground. “I don’t belong here. I’m really Jeff Thompson dammit! I’m not Tracy Mason, I’m a man! You can’t do this!” The crying woman regained her composure enough to squeak out to you “Don’t worry honey were gonna get you all the help you need, don’t fight it.” Struggling with all your might under these living restraints you fight to get free. As you struggle your hopes and dreams run ice cold seeing one of the men now holding you retrieve a syringe from the van and pop off the cap, aiming it for a vein on your wrist. Now screaming and shrieking you plead for anyone to help you from the crowd of neighbors that had formed around you but none would budge. Soon you yourself began crying again and shouting “Please no don’t do this, oh god please” as the needle entered your vein and the contents were injected. Two weeks later and the professionals of the Pullman Institute were just as stumped as before when the patient was first wheeled in. The meeting between the Doctor, Mr. Thompson and his girlfriend Emma Lockhart was quick and to the point as both seemed normal and well-adjusted teenagers, deeply in love, and having no idea why Ms. Mason was so distraught.

The last two weeks had been a whirlwind of treatment activity with Ms. Mason ranging from mood stabilizers, and anti-depressants, to corrective behavior therapy and even hypnosis but she refused to drop her persona that she was really Jeffery Thompson trapped in Tracy Mason’s body. Every treatment had been tried and even though it was incredibly risky to the mental stability of the patient long-term her distraught and exhausted parents who Ms. Mason wouldn’t even acknowledge with much heartache signed off for electric shock therapy. Being just under eighteen years old was a lucky break for her parents for if she was over eighteen they wouldn’t be able to do the therapy without the young Ms Mason’s approval. Four Months Later:

“After many months of daily sessions I as her doctor am pleased to announce that I believe Ms. Mason has been cured of her original mental ailment and is now on solid enough psychological grounds to be released immediately.” Those were the words that the head of Tracy Mason’s treatment at the Pullman Institute Dr Adam Venski had told Mr. and Mrs. Mason during a meeting in his office four months to the day since they had begun the daily behavioral treatment sessions involving electric shock. The patient Ms. Mason was combative at first but her resistance had been broken by the middle of the second week. After this break progress had been rapid and at the end of the first month she barely had any hangups in her memory or acceptance of herself. The patient eagerly accepted who she was as well as her gender and femininity but required additional sessions of both the above mentioned methods as well as teaching and workshop sessions with female staff in order to learn how to be and act female again. The reteaching points to possible amnesia or brain damage due to her earlier mental break but more research is needed before a judgment can be made on this hypothesis. Ms. Masons parents were ecstatic about the news and that they were finally going to have their girl back with them. Taking them into the waiting room and sitting them down I called up the orderly assigned to her room to bring Tracy out to be with her parents.

Several Minutes Earlier:

“I could never understand why this knot in my hair always seemed to form after I took a shower and had dried my hair with the towel?” “I can’t wait until I’m allowed to have a blow dryer” I thought as I sat at my desk and combed by long luscious hair under the watchful eye of the orderly. I loved how soft and straight my hair was and I was so thankful to get my mothers hair genes and gender in order to enjoy combing and styling it everyday. Something was going on today unlike the usual for not only was I allowed a longer supervised shower but I was also allowed to apply makeup they had temporary given me and which I squealed with joy at being able to apply. I was also allowed grooming tools such as a hair comb to straightened my look out. I was told to dress in a very cute knee-length yellow sundress that they had laid on my bed and which I also took great joy in donning and modeling to the plastic makeup mirror. I think I knew what was happening and I was bubbling with joy sitting on the bed in my dress thinking about finally going home back to my life, family, and friends.

Soon enough the orderly which had earlier retreated outside my room now reentered and with a smile on his face announced that my parents were here and I would be allowed to go see them in the waiting room. Knowing what that meant I jumped up in the air off the bed and squealed with delight. Told to follow him down the hall I nearly ran ahead of him as I was led into the waiting room to my crying mother and smiling father waiting for me. The resulting hugs and tears threatened to go on forever as even I couldn’t help leaking water and mascara down my cheeks during my apologies to my parents and my everlasting love of them and their patience. My parents lovingly scolding me for ever thinking what happened was my fault and that I will always be their little girl who they loved dearly. After the waterworks had been turned off and we had calmed down enough to talk a smiling Dr. Venski who was standing to the side of our group had announced that I was cured and would be able to go home immediately. Hearing this news I was ecstatic with joy which of course did nothing to prevent myself from crying again as my parents moved in for more hugs to add to the joyous news. The move out was quick and to the point from there as I only had a small bag of items which I was allowed to own in the institute and not much else to my name. Thanking Dr. Venski profusely for giving me my sense of self back really came from the heart as I was who I was supposed to be again and would never again imagine entertaining any type of insane thought such as myself really being a boy let alone the boyfriend of my best friend.

Ultimately this translated into a lucrative modeling contract with one of the top modeling agencies in the country. Five years after this string of good luck Tracy Mason was hailed by every fashion mogul and media source to be one of the top supermodels in the world appearing in major swimwear brand campaigns and runway shoots, along Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Edition magazine multiple years in a row, and Victoria’s Secret catalog. Tracy loved her life and what it had allowed her to achieve through modeling. The good times continued to roll for Tracy Mason for before passing the age of 25 she had married an award-winning Hollywood actor who allowed her by the time she was 27 to enter motherhood. Tracy would continue to model while raising her family with her husband before retiring at 38 years old. In the end Tracy would embrace motherhood and married life having a total of four children. Laying in bed at night soon after she had gotten married and now on her dream honeymoon Tracy lay snuggled up right beside her man and gripped in his strong embrace. Tracy thought back to her high school years and start to giggle at how she had ever come up with the crazy idea that she was ever a man.
